ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Continued To May-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-87-247348 The Estate of Pearl Smith and PES-12-296291 The Estate of Willie Smith.

Appearances: Roger Gross for Richard Smith (Executor) and Dwight Smith (Administrator).

In Re: The Pearl Smith Estate - The Court granted the Petition for Instructions Re Ratification of Distribution Agreement Among Beneficiaries, etc. (subject to review of the order).

In Re: The Willie Smith Estate - Mr. Gross stated that there is an inaccuracy in his fee request, that it should be $12,800.00, that it has been corrected in the first and second amendments and that this petition has been on calendar for two years. Mr. Gross also stated that they asked for a $15,000.00 reserve because taxes needed to be prepared, that there were real estate taxes, that they have all been paid and cleared, that there is no need for a reserve and asked that any remaining funds be applied to his extraordinary fees. In addition, Mr. Gross stated that he advised his Client to do the cashier's checks because his Client was being deployed, that there wasn't enough Court staff at the time and the hearing date was after his Client's deployment and that his Client left the checks with his Mother. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to May 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Gross to amend the petition.

Judge: John Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Reinstate Administrator With Full Authority Under The Independent Administration Of Estates Act
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for petitioner Dwight Smith; Shashauna Szczechowicz (by CourtCall) for Surtec.

Mr. Gross informed the Court that the verification has been signed, and that he will file it when he receives the signed original. The Court asked if anyone was objecting to the petition. Ms. Szczechowicz stated that Surtec had no objection. The matter was taken under submission.

Mr. Gross informed the Court that there was a pending hearing date of November 9, 2016 on his client's amended third petition. He stated that he was in the process of drafting a memorandum of points and authorities for that petition, and requested that the hearing date be continued to a later date to give him time to complete the submission of his memorandum. The Court granted the request, and ordered the continuance of that hearing to December 12, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Continued To Apr-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for (petitioner) Major Dwight O. Smith. Shashauna Szczechowicz for Suretec Insurance Company.

Mr. Gross stated that Major Smith is on telephone standby and clarified that Richard Smith is a beneficiary of this estate and the executor of the Carrie Smith estate. The Court asked about the documentation regarding Major Smith's injuries and the delay with communication. Mr. Gross provided police and medical reports to the court, reported that Major Smith had been in a car accident the day after he returned from Afghanistan, that he is in Virginia and receiving physical therapy, that he has serious injuries and described them, that some distributions were improperly made and that he is working with his client and the family to resolve the issues. The Court returned the reports to Mr. Gross and requested that he redact the personal information and file the reports with the court. Mr. Gross stated that they are hoping to continue this matter to April 11, 2016, that they will have a stipulation filed by the family, that they are working on the distribution amounts that were improper, that they are hoping that the court will not require a re-distribution, that the heirs will be stipulating to an agreement, that there are issues pending with the taxes, that Major Smith can now address those issues but will need his suspension lifted to do so and explained to the court why Major Smith had been suspended. The Court stated that it will reinstate Major Smith, that Mr. Gross is to submit a proposed order stating that it is necessary to reinstate Major Smith to deal with the tax issues and that his powers will be limited powers for tax preparation. Ms. Szczechowicz stated that the surety is in support of the reappointment to close out the estate. The Court asked if Major Smith needed to be re-appointed for all purposes and Mr. Gross responded that it was not necessary. Ms. Szczechowicz stated that by the next hearing date they will have a clear view of what is needed. The accounting petition was continued to April 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Macksoud. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Continued To Feb-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for Dwight Smith.

The Court stated that it was disappointed that Major Smith was not in attendance. Mr. Gross stated that Major Smith returned in December, that he has been trying to communicate with him but is not getting a response, that he emailed Major Smith with the issues regarding the estate, that due to the reopening of the Carrie Smith estate they have had to go back and forth, that because he had no way of communicating with Major Smith he recommended that Richard Smith be appointed in the Carrie Smith case, that there are family issues, that he wrote a letter last week to Major Smith that if he and the family wish to object to the appointment in the Carrie Smith estate the matter could be continued for such and that he does not know if the funds are there. The Court stated that it wishes to suspend Dwight Smith today. Mr. Gross asked for a 4 week continuance to be able to let Major Smith know that this is his window of opportunity. The Court stated that it would be suspending Dwight Smith as this has been going on for over a year. The accounting petition was continued to February 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for Dwight Smith.

Mr. Gross reported that his client was in the U.S. on leave back East, that he promised to send the accounting with the checks but that he has not received such, that there are family issues being played out by various family members, that Mr. Gross does not know what the issues/problems are, that he has told his client that this estate needs to be closed, that Major Smith will be back in December but the exact date is classified, that he would like to continue this matter until his client can make an appearance and that he can advise the court of his return date when it is known. The Court asked if an amended petition was needed or if there was anything that the court would be able to get ahead of time. Mr. Gross stated that there is a side issue that is preventing this from being completed, that he gets conflicting information from family members, that his client has demanded that he withdraw from the Carrie Smith estate and that he will not do so until this is settled. The accounting petition was continued to January 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Gross is to contact the court if Major Smith returns earlier.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Continued To Aug-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross, for Petitioner Dwight O. Smith.
Mr. Gross reported that his client is a Major in the U.S. Army, who has been deployed to Afghanistan since the end of February. Prior to that deployment, Mr. Gross had advised Major Smith to obtain cashier's checks for the anticipated estate distributions, with directions that the payments not be distributed until the Court so ordered. In his preparations for his deployment, Major Smith gave the cashier's checks to his mother. Mr. Gross later learned that Major Smith's mother had given a distribution check to her daughter, contrary to his directions. Mr. Gross has attempted to communicate with Major Smith by email during the deployment, but has received no response, and he does not know if his emails have been received. He has written to Major Smith's mother and requested her to send him all accounting information regarding estate funds, with no response received. He has also written to her daughter requesting that she return the premature distribution, with no response. Major Smith's mother informed him that she does not have the time to act as administrator of the estate, and that she has written to the Army requesting that it end Major Smith's deployment overseas so that he can return to resume the estate's administration. Mr. Gross stated that he does not expect the Army to comply with that request. The Court inquired whether Major Smith should continue as the representative of the estate. Mr. Gross responded that Richard Smith has served as executor of the Carrie Smith estate, and that he could potentially serve in that capacity for the Willie Smith estate as well. He would like to speak with Richard Smith to confirm his willingness to serve, in which case he will prepare a petition to substitute him in as the estate representative. Mr. Gross requested that the Court set a status hearing in three weeks to explore that option. The matter was continued to August 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, for a status hearing.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Continued To Apr-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for (petitioner) Dwight Smith.

The court stated that the petition seeks to distribute to the heirs of a post deceased wife directly and that it should be distributed to her estate to handle. Mr. Gross cited an omnibus clause allowing post distribution, stated that two of the heirs that are now deceased had no children and that they would like to close the estate prior to Major Smith being deployed to Afghanistan this month. The court stated that the estate of Carrie Smith will need to be re-opened to be able to proceed, that the order will not be signed until there is an estate to distribute to, and pointed out that there is an issue with notice - that the proof of service must be on the mandatory form. The accounting petition was continued to April 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on re-opening the Carrie Smith estate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
